titleStmt.title: SII 3.74: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
VI.—INSCRIPTIONS OF KULOTTUNGA-CHOLA I. No. 74.—INSCRIPTION IN THE PANDAVA-PERUMAL TEMPLE.

summary.p: This inscription (No. 18 of 1893) is engraved on the south wall of the Pāṇḍava-Perumāḷ temple at Conjeeveram. As in No. 68 above, the name of the temple is given as Tiruppāḍagam (l. 3).

The date is the 39th year of the reign of Kulōttuṅga I. But the historical introduction agrees almost literally with an inscription of the 5th year in the same temple (No. 68 above), while the intervening inscriptions (Nos. 69 to 73, 78, and Vol. II. No. 58) contain much additional matter.

The inscription records that a merchant of Kāñchipuram paid two kaḻañju and two mañjāḍi of gold to the Pūjāris of the temple, who pledged themselves to have the god supplied daily with two nāḻi of curds.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . pukaḻ cūḻn=ta puṇari Akaḻ cūḻn=ta puviyil pon=nemiyaḷavun=tan=nemi naṭātti viḷaṅku jayamakaḷ Iḷaṅko paruvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramattoḻilāl putumaṇam puṇarn=tantu vaṉkaḷiṟṟiīṭṭam vaiyirākarattu vaāri AiAyilmunaikkon=taḷavaraicar tan=tanamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittu toḷ vali kāṭṭi porppari naṭātti kīrttiyai niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūṭi ten=ticai temarukamalappūmakaḷ pupotumaiyum pon=niyāṭaiyum nan=nilappāvaiyun=taiṉ tanimaiyun=tavirttu punṉitaruttirumaṇimakuṭam muṟaimaiyil cūṭi tan=naṭi Iraṇṭum taṭamuṭiyāka
tonnilaven=tar cūṭa ponni matuvāṟu peruka kaliyāṟu vaṟappa ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñcella veṇkuṭai Irunilavaḷākameṅkaṇun=tanātu tirunilaveṇṇilāttikaḻa Orutani meruvil puli viḷaiyāṭa Āḻkaṭal tiīvān=tarattu pūvar tiṟai viṭutta kalaṉ cori kaḷuiṟu muṟai niṟpa viḷaṅkiya ten=navanarun=talai parun=talaittu kiṭappa naṉmaṇiyāramun=ti ruppuyattalaṅkalum tan=tṉatu vīramum tiyākamum viḷaṅka pārmicai mevalar vaṇaṅka vīrasiṁhāsanattu Avanimuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viīṟṟiru
n=taruḷiya kovirājakesaripanmarāna Uṭaiyār śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevarkku yāṇṭu muppattoṉpatāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu Eyirkṟkoṭṭattu nakaraṅkāñcipurattu tiruppāṭakattāḻvānaittiruvārātanai paṇṇum bhāraradvācji ninṟanārāyaṇapaṭṭanenum dūdakari tiruvaraṅkamāṇipaṭṭanenum cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu . In=nakarattu rājāśriayapperun=teruvil Irukkum vāṇiyaṉ toṭṭaṅkiḻānātan coman pakkal nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa maturān=takamāṭai
yoṭokkum poṉ kuṭiñaikkal niṟai Irukaḻañce Iraṇṭu mañcāṭikkum policaiyāl Ivvāḻvānukku nittappaṭikku ninṟān=nnāl Irunāḻi taiyiramutu ceykaikku celuttakkaṭavomākavumāṭaṭomākila celuttuvār pakkalippoṉ kuṭukka kaṭavomākavum . Ikkoyil kāṇi tavirn=tu pokillippoṉ Oṭukkippokakkaṭavomānom Ivviruvom ..

(Line 3.) In the thirty-ninth year (of the reign) of king Rājakēsarivarman, alias the lord Śrī-Kulōttuṅga-Śōḻadēva, who etc.The introduction of this inscription is the same as in No. 68 above. —the following writing on stone was made and given by me, Niṉṟanārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭaṉ of the Bhāradvāja (gōtra) and by me, Dū[da]kariThis is the Tamil spelling of Dūta-Hari, i.e. ‘Kṛishṇa as messenger (of the Pāṇḍavas).’ With it may be connected Pāṇḍavadūta-Perumāḷ, which is given by Mr. Kanakasabhai Pillai (Ind. Ant). Vol. XXI p. 281) as the name of the Pāṇḍava-Perumāḷ temple. Tiruvaraṅgamāṇi-Bhaṭṭaṉ, who are performing the worship in the temple of Tiruppāḍagatt-Āḻvāṉ at Kāñchipuram, a city in Eyiṟkōṭṭam,See above, Vol. II. p. 390. (a district) of Jayaṅgoṇḍa-Śōḻa-maṇḍalam. From Tōṭṭaṅgiḻānādaṉ Sōmaṉ, a merchant who resides in the great street of RājāśrayaThis was one of the surnames of Rājarāja I.; see above, Vol. II. p. 260, note 5. in this city, we have received two kaḻañju and two mañjāḍi of gold, weighed by the standard of the city (kuḍiñai-kal) (and) equal (in fineness) to the Madurāntaka-māḍai.On kuḍiñai-kal and Madurāntaka-māḍai see Ep. Ind. Vol. V. p. 106, notes 1 and 3.

(L. 4.) Out of the interest from (this) we shall be bound to pay for supplying daily to this god two nāḻi of curds by the Niṉṟāṉ (? measure). If we are not able (to pay it), we shall be bound to make over this gold to those who will pay (it). If (our) right (to serve) in this temple should cease, we two shall be bound to refund this money before leaving.
Digital edition of SII 3.74 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
